options.explanation_en: Overfitting occurs when a model learns the noise in training data and fails to generalize. The issue here is an imbalance in data distribution, not excessive model adaptation.
Concept drift occurs when data distribution or model objectives change over time. This situation involves a sampling composition problem at the time of training data collection.
Data leakage occurs when test data information is included in training data or target prediction information is embedded in training features. This case represents data selection bias.

Training data is skewed toward specific industries while other industries are insufficiently represented. This is a typical sampling bias, where the model learns a distorted data distribution, resulting in unfair predictions for underrepresented groups.
Accuracy measures the proportion of correct predictions among all predictions in classification problems. It is unsuitable for evaluating machine translation quality.

BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) is the international standard for evaluating machine translation quality. Based on n-gram word matching, it automatically measures similarity between generated and reference translations and is optimized for version comparison and performance improvement tracking.
F1 Score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall, used for evaluating imbalanced classification problems. It is not suitable for translation quality assessment.
AUC-ROC measures the performance of binary classification models by the area under the ROC curve. It is unsuitable for evaluating machine translation quality.
S3 is a data storage service and Glue is a data integration tool; neither offers automatic sentiment analysis or text generation capabilities.
Rekognition is an image and video analysis service, and Polly is a text-to-speech service. They do not provide text sentiment analysis or automated report generation.
EBS is a block storage service and CloudWatch is a monitoring service; neither provides text analysis or generation capabilities.
Comprehend provides text sentiment analysis and entity extraction, while Bedrock enables foundation model-based text generation, fulfilling all requirements.
Decision trees provide clear rule-based decision paths, enabling step-by-step explanation of how each feature influences the final decision with superior explainability.
PCA is a dimensionality reduction technique used to compress high-dimensional data into lower dimensions. It is not directly used for classification or explaining decision-making processes.
While DNNs provide high prediction accuracy, they operate through numerous hidden layers and are considered black boxes, making it difficult to clearly explain the reasoning behind decisions.
K-means is an unsupervised learning algorithm used to partition data into groups. It is not suitable for binary or multiclass classification problems.

options.text_en: Switch to a more efficient foundation model while maintaining the current prompt length.
options.explanation_en: Model switching alone does not reduce the number of input tokens, limiting cost savings.

options.text_en: Store generated legal documents in Amazon S3 for reuse in future requests.
options.explanation_en: This approach incurs additional storage costs and requires generating new documents for each case, making substantial cost reduction difficult.

options.text_en: Reduce the number of precedents and guidelines included in each API call prompt to decrease input tokens.
options.explanation_en: This is the most effective method to directly reduce input tokens. Removing unnecessary precedents and streamlining guidelines achieves both quality maintenance and cost savings.

options.text_en: Reduce API call frequency and batch process multiple document requests into a single call.
options.explanation_en: Batch processing changes the processing approach only; input tokens per document remain unchanged, limiting overall cost reduction.
